evals.prompt: Add an Epoch pay button to my React app that sends 0.15 USDC on Base to a fixed address. I use wagmi already.
evals.expected_output: A component rendering EpochIntentWidget in pay mode with flat props, plus a note to import styles.css and wrap in WagmiProvider + QueryClientProvider.
expectations: Renders <EpochIntentWidget /> controlled by isOpen/onClose — does not reimplement connect/quote/sign/poll
Uses mode="pay" with flat props (toAddress, toAmount as a decimal string, toChainId, toToken)
Passes api={{ baseUrl }}
Imports '@epoch-protocol/epoch-intent-widget/styles.css' at the app root
Mentions WagmiProvider + QueryClientProvider are required
Does NOT use bigint for the flat toAmount prop

evals.prompt: Integrate an Epoch swap widget where the user can pick both tokens, default destination 1 USDC on Base.
evals.expected_output: EpochIntentWidget in swap mode with a nested intent using protocol 'swap', fixedOutput true, requiredAmount as bigint atomic units.
expectations: Uses mode="swap"
Passes a nested intent={{ … }} with config.protocol = 'swap'
requiredAmount is a bigint in atomic units (e.g. 1_000_000n for 1 USDC, decimals 6)
Does not set lockDestinationToken (or notes it is forced off in swap)
Passes api={{ baseUrl }}

evals.prompt: Add an Epoch earn/lending flow with live 1delta markets on Ethereum and Base, Aave and Morpho only.
evals.expected_output: EpochIntentWidget in earn mode with positionsBaseUrl set, earnChainIds and earnLenderFilter scoped.
expectations: Uses mode="earn"
Sets api.positionsBaseUrl for live data
Sets earnChainIds={[1, 8453]}
Sets earnLenderFilter to a CSV like "AAVE_V3,MORPHO"
Notes Earn is mainnet-only

evals.prompt: I want Epoch's pay logic but my own custom UI, no widget. How?
evals.expected_output: Use the headless EpochFlowsSDK / PaySession with createPaySession, subscribe via .on(), submit(), and dispose().
expectations: Uses EpochFlowsSDK and createPaySession (or PaySession) instead of the widget component
Subscribes to events with session.on(...) and reads that .on returns an unsubscribe function
Calls session.submit(...) and session.dispose() on teardown
Passes a viem WalletClient and the user address
Does not reimplement quoting/polling by hand
